meta.certificationName: Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer
meta.title: GCP-MLE-1: Vertex AI Pipelines, Serving and Responsible AI

questions.stem: A team needs to **manage reusable online and offline ML features**. Which service or feature is the best fit?

options.explanation: It is the managed Vertex AI feature repository for training and online serving use cases
It is not the managed Vertex AI service for end-to-end pipeline orchestration
It is not the Vertex AI feature intended for deployed-model drift monitoring
They are not the Vertex AI option for large offline scoring jobs

questions.stem:
Which service or feature is most directly associated with the following need?

- Goal: manage reusable online and offline ML features
- Constraint: use the managed vendor capability when possible

What should the team choose?

options.explanation: They are not the Vertex AI option for large offline scoring jobs
It is the managed Vertex AI feature repository for training and online serving use cases
It is not the Vertex AI feature intended for deployed-model drift monitoring
It is not the managed Vertex AI service for end-to-end pipeline orchestration

questions.stem: A team must explain **which features most influenced predictions** from a tabular classification model on Vertex AI. Which Google Cloud capability is the best fit?

options.explanation: Vertex Explainable AI provides feature-attribution explanations for supported models so teams can understand which inputs influenced predictions.
Batch prediction generates offline predictions, not explanations.
Pipelines orchestrates workflow steps but does not itself produce model explanations.
Cloud Logging stores logs, not feature-attribution analysis.

questions.stem: A model has been deployed to a Vertex AI endpoint. The team wants to detect **training-serving skew and prediction drift** after release. Which capability should they implement?

options.explanation: Vertex AI Model Monitoring is designed to monitor deployed models for drift and skew in production.

options.text: Vertex Explainable AI only during training
options.explanation: Explainability alone does not continuously monitor serving drift or skew.
Workbench is for notebook-based development, not production model monitoring.
Cloud Scheduler triggers jobs but does not provide model-drift detection.

questions.stem: A product team wants to compare two prompt strategies with **automatic metrics and optional human judgment** before rolling changes into production. Which Google Cloud capability best matches this requirement?

options.explanation: Vertex AI evaluation supports structured model and prompt evaluation workflows using automatic metrics and, for some workflows, human judgment.
Feature Store manages reusable features rather than evaluating prompt quality.
Online prediction serves live requests but does not compare prompt strategies.
BigQuery ML remote models are not the primary Vertex AI evaluation workflow asked for here.

questions.stem: A review board asks for **documented responsible AI practices across the ML lifecycle**, including governance considerations beyond a single API feature. Which resource should the team consult first?

options.explanation: Google Cloud Responsible AI guidance summarizes principles, lifecycle practices, and governance considerations that apply across ML systems rather than a single serving feature.
Batch predictions run inference jobs and do not provide responsible AI governance guidance.
Custom training runs code but is not the cross-cutting governance resource requested.
Storage Transfer Service moves data and is unrelated to responsible AI policy guidance.
